narration: The cashier just asked — 'do you want the protection plan?' And statistically, you said yes. That was probably a $399 mistake.
visual: SVG cash register with glowing screen showing 'PROTECTION PLAN? ADD FOR $399?'; animated red exclamation popup springs in; large 'THAT YES COSTS YOU THOUSANDS' text fades in; red badge '$40 BILLION SCAM / YEAR'

narration: Americans spend $40 billion a year on extended warranties — more than we spend on pet food. And about 80% of that goes straight to the retailer as pure profit.
visual: Two animated bar charts growing upward side by side: red bar labeled WARRANTIES $40B grows taller than gray bar labeled PET FOOD $30B; red callout AND 80% IS PURE PROFIT FOR THE RETAILER fades in

narration: Here's the brutal math — only 12% of extended warranties are ever used. That means 88 cents of every dollar you hand over goes directly into the retailer's pocket.
visual: Donut chart animating from empty to 12% filled red (EVER USED) with 88% gray background; large '88¢ OF EVERY DOLLAR TO PROFIT' text fades in; bottom callout '88% ARE NEVER CLAIMED — IT'S THE BUSINESS MODEL'

narration: Here's why you keep saying yes. Your brain fears loss way more than it values gain — and retailers engineered that checkout question to trigger exactly that fear.
visual: Person silhouette with fear thought bubble 'WHAT IF IT BREAKS?!'; beside it a math panel showing ACTUAL ODDS: 1 IN 8 with a 12.5% progress bar; red callout THEY ENGINEERED THAT FEAR RIGHT AT CHECKOUT

narration: What they don't tell you — most premium credit cards already include free purchase protection for one to two years. And the money you'd spend on warranties? Put it in a repair fund and keep what you don't use.
visual: Credit card SVG with red shield badge showing free protection; piggy bank SVG beside it with $400/YR redirected label growing in; red outlined callout MOST CARDS PROTECT YOU FREE

narration: Skip every extended warranty over your lifetime and you keep over $8,000. Next time they ask at checkout, you know exactly what to say.
visual: Large '$8K+' number growing with spring animation; person silhouette with green money bag; red banner NEXT TIME THEY ASK: JUST SAY NO; dark CTA banner FOLLOW FOR MORE MONEY MATH
